findings.reason: Current-head scans and rendered pages confirmed first-party credential implications; public copy now describes Midwest Roots without an ISA credential and uses independent credential referrals only.

acceptance_results.criterion: No page, metadata, schema, manifest, or social copy implies an active ISA credential
acceptance_results.evidence: Current source scan has no Midwest Roots certified-arborist claim; manifests were removed; rendered metadata was inspected.

acceptance_results.criterion: Any third-party credential includes holder, credential, verification link, and role
acceptance_results.evidence: No specific third-party credential is published; generic referrals explicitly say independently credentialed and make no holder claim.

notes: No active ISA credential is attributed to the company or owner.

findings.reason: The current head no longer mounted FastQuoteWidget, but the legacy GET receipt remained public and was contained. Final production once-only receipt remains external CONV-003 evidence.

acceptance_results.criterion: No success state is reachable without acknowledged persistence or delivery
acceptance_results.evidence: Legacy GET renders Finish Your Estimate Request; form success requires a first-party receipt.

acceptance_results.criterion: Until durable lead acceptance exists, the fast-quote path routes into the real contact flow and never promises follow-up from ZIP/service alone
acceptance_results.evidence: Dormant widget routes to /contact and legacy URL states ZIP/service alone do not submit.

acceptance_results.criterion: A labeled test lead arrives once with source, service, ZIP, contact, timestamp, and receipt ID after final closure
acceptance_results.evidence: Local accepted/duplicate behavior passed, but destination arrival requires production store/provider/inbox access.

acceptance_results.criterion: Direct GET cannot display a receipt
acceptance_results.evidence: Playwright direct-GET regression passed.

notes: Containment files are attributed to CONV-002 because blocked records cannot list changed files under schema v2.

findings.reason: Browser EmailJS was replaced by a server-owned schema, durable file-backed receipt/idempotency store, pending queue state, authenticated delivery attempt, and fail-closed UI.

acceptance_results.criterion: Submission has a stable receipt ID
acceptance_results.evidence: 201 response and persisted UUID receipt verified locally.

acceptance_results.criterion: Duplicate requests are idempotent
acceptance_results.evidence: Same idempotency key returned the same receipt with duplicate=true; Vitest covers durable duplicate lookup.

acceptance_results.criterion: Provider outage produces queued/retry or explicit failure
acceptance_results.evidence: Accepted record remains delivery=pending on absent/failed webhook with operator signal; missing durable store returns homeowner-safe 503.

acceptance_results.criterion: No provider key is shipped to browsers
acceptance_results.evidence: @emailjs/browser and NEXT_PUBLIC EmailJS configuration were removed; delivery token is server-only.

notes: Production durable storage and retry scheduling remain release configuration gates, not hidden success claims.

findings.reason: Next.js was patched from 16.2.1 to 16.2.6, clearing applicable high/critical direct production advisories.

acceptance_results.criterion: npm audit has no applicable high/critical production advisory
acceptance_results.evidence: Final npm audit --omit=dev reports four moderate findings and zero high/critical.

acceptance_results.criterion: Build and defining workflows pass on patched version
acceptance_results.evidence: 60-page Next 16.2.6 production build and three Playwright defining tests passed.

notes: Next's nested PostCSS advisory remains moderate and has no nonbreaking upstream resolution in the installed release.

findings.reason: Vitest characterization/failure coverage, production-server Playwright journeys, and a pull-request quality workflow were added without weakening the unsafe historical contract.

acceptance_results.criterion: Characterization coverage captures the false-receipt, active hazard scoring, current range selection, and photo-mailto behavior before risky replacement
acceptance_results.evidence: Retained teardown evidence established historical behaviors; regression tests now prove direct GET containment, scoring thresholds, broad range UI, and manual mail without a file input.

acceptance_results.criterion: CI runs deterministic tests for five tools and the lead lifecycle
acceptance_results.evidence: Quality workflow runs Vitest plus production Playwright; local Playwright exercised all five tool routes.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tests cover invalid, boundary, failure, duplicate, and mobile navigation paths
acceptance_results.evidence: Schema size/contact boundaries, no-store 503, duplicate receipt, oversize 413, mobile menu/skip/overflow passed.

acceptance_results.criterion: No test weakens or normalizes an unsafe or misleading current contract
acceptance_results.evidence: Tests assert false receipt absent, hazard uncertainty present, dangerous vehicle-pull copy absent, and photo picker absent.

notes: Firefox/WebKit and real AT stay under A11Y-001 rather than being overstated as CI coverage.

findings.reason: Owner selected rewrite/remove; guarantees, licensing/insurance, equipment, experience, volume, quantified performance, and response promises were removed or bounded.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner supplies evidence or selects replace/remove for every claims-inventory row mapped to TRUST-002
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner explicitly selected retain only with evidence or rewrite/remove; current scan and rendered review found no retained unsupported mapped claim.

acceptance_results.criterion: Insurance/licensing claims identify jurisdiction, status, and verification where applicable
acceptance_results.evidence: No public insurance or licensing claim remains.

acceptance_results.criterion: Guarantee terms are written, bounded, and available before purchase
acceptance_results.evidence: Unsupported zero-damage and implied outcome guarantees were removed; no guarantee is offered.

acceptance_results.criterion: Quantified statistics and response promises have an owner, source period, and update cadence
acceptance_results.evidence: Unsubstantiated statistics, since-2023 claim, operating hours, response times, and performance percentages were removed rather than retained.

notes: Authority-heavy prior copy was treated as implementation debt, not owner intent.

findings.reason: Questions, scoring, result depth, urgency, safety guidance, next steps, and conversion remain; conclusions now report only the homeowner's answers and limitations.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner records option A (conservative screener) or option B (credential-reviewed rebuild); recommendation is option A unless an active independent credential holder accepts review responsibility
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner selected conservative preliminary homeowner screening.

acceptance_results.criterion: No “official,” “certified method,” “diagnosis,” or ISA-framework claim
acceptance_results.evidence: Active component and public copy scan contain none of these authority claims.

acceptance_results.criterion: Emergency red flags give keep-away and appropriate emergency/utility instructions
acceptance_results.evidence: Extreme result directs keep-away, no work beneath tree, emergency services for immediate danger, and utility for line contact.

acceptance_results.criterion: All results state limits and route formal assessment to qualified help
acceptance_results.evidence: Every score narrative is tied to reported signs; low result explicitly does not establish soundness; CTA routes on-site help.

notes: Independent professional review remains a release-strengthening gate, not a claim that review occurred.

findings.reason: Ladder, chainsaw, vehicle pull, felling, overhead cutting, and utility instructions were removed/reclassified, but named independent safety review has not occurred.

acceptance_results.criterion: No homeowner instruction recommends ladder, chainsaw, vehicle pulling, climbing, overhead cutting, or work near utilities
acceptance_results.evidence: Current DIY tool prohibits or reclassifies these tasks; Playwright asserts vehicle-pull copy absent.

acceptance_results.criterion: Safety copy has named outside review date/owner
acceptance_results.evidence: No independently qualified reviewer has been engaged; the site and artifact preserve this release gate.

notes: Safety edits are attributed to TRUST-002/CONTENT-002 because schema-blocked findings cannot list changed files.

findings.reason: UI now states educational possibilities, uncertainty, no diagnosis, and urgent referral, but material biological/treatment entries still require authoritative sourcing and dated professional review.

acceptance_results.criterion: Each material biological/treatment claim has source and review date
acceptance_results.evidence: Independent sources and a qualified review owner/date are not available for every retained entry.

acceptance_results.criterion: Outputs use uncertainty language and distinguish identification from diagnosis
acceptance_results.evidence: Species matching and ailment reference explicitly deny confirmed identification/diagnosis and warn not to treat from an entry alone.

acceptance_results.criterion: Urgent triggers route appropriately without asserting diagnosis
acceptance_results.evidence: Urgent CTA refers to warning signs/reference entry and requests on-site review without saying the ailment was diagnosed.

notes: Related UI changes are attributed to TRUST-002/CONTENT-002 while professional content review remains blocked.

findings.reason: Owner supplied retention/access/deletion choices; a linked privacy notice covers current first-party records and honest manual-photo email with no reuse opt-in.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner records a specific retention duration for first-party lead records
acceptance_results.evidence: 12 months for unconverted leads is encoded, displayed, and documented.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner defines routine deletion, deletion-request handling, and any legal/operational hold exception
acceptance_results.evidence: Privacy page and runbook define delete/anonymize, request steps, and bounded active-customer/dispute/security/business-record holds.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner names the roles or individuals permitted to access stored lead records and the access-review cadence
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner and explicitly authorized operators only; changes and quarterly review documented.

acceptance_results.criterion: Privacy page is linked at every collection point
acceptance_results.evidence: Both forms link it and global footer exposes it; photo workflow collects no file or consent.

acceptance_results.criterion: Photo reuse is unchecked and separable
acceptance_results.evidence: Reuse checkbox was removed; sending email explicitly grants no reuse.

acceptance_results.criterion: Retention/deletion contact and processors are named
acceptance_results.evidence: Privacy page names owner contact and current Vercel hosting/analytics; future owner-selected delivery/email provider must be privately documented before configuration.

notes: No claim of compliance with a named legal regime is made.

findings.reason: Page titles no longer embed the global brand template redundantly; each tool has generated accurate title, description, and canonical metadata.

acceptance_results.criterion: Rendered titles are unique, accurate, and not double-branded
acceptance_results.evidence: Title sources were normalized to rely on the root template; rendered contact title and build routes inspected.

acceptance_results.criterion: All five tools have canonical and accurate descriptions
acceptance_results.evidence: generateMetadata maps all five slugs to bounded titles/descriptions/canonicals.

findings.reason: Owner chose to keep useful local coverage and obvious credential assertions were removed, but query/lead evidence and owner substantiation for every unique local assertion are unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner decides retain/consolidate based on query and lead evidence
acceptance_results.evidence: Retain-useful direction is recorded, but Search Console/query/lead evidence was not available to select consolidation candidates.

acceptance_results.criterion: Every retained page has verified unique content and clear conversion path
acceptance_results.evidence: Routes build and conversion links exist; full owner verification of each local assertion remains unavailable.

notes: No mass deletion was performed; baseline neighborhood work was preserved.

findings.reason: Skip link, one-H1 tool route semantics, and active hazard/cost focus were repaired; Firefox/WebKit and real NVDA/VoiceOver gates remain unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: One H1 per tool route
acceptance_results.evidence: Dynamic route supplies one slug-specific H1; production pages built.

acceptance_results.criterion: Skip link is first focusable control and visible on focus
acceptance_results.evidence: Playwright mobile keyboard test proves first Tab focuses the skip link.

acceptance_results.criterion: Results receive programmatic focus or appropriate live announcement
acceptance_results.evidence: Hazard and cost headings receive focus; result containers expose live/result markers.

acceptance_results.criterion: Supported Firefox/WebKit journeys verify keyboard entry, focus order, result announcement, and recovery without engine-specific failure
acceptance_results.evidence: Only installed Chrome was executable; Firefox/WebKit were not available.

acceptance_results.criterion: At least one real NVDA or VoiceOver pass verifies the defining tool and lead journeys; unavailable engines or assistive technology remain an explicit release gate
acceptance_results.evidence: No real NVDA/VoiceOver environment was available; accessibility page and handoff retain the gate.

notes: Accessibility edits are attributed to SEO-001/TEST-001 while this evidence-complete finding remains blocked.

findings.reason: No-account access, five routes, phone fallback, interaction depth, and narrow/wide responsive behavior were preserved.

acceptance_results.criterion: Core tools remain usable without account
acceptance_results.evidence: All five production routes returned 200 and defining Playwright journeys required no account.

acceptance_results.criterion: 390px and 1440px layouts have no horizontal overflow
acceptance_results.evidence: 390px Playwright overflow assertion passed; 390 screenshot and 1440 Chrome render completed.

acceptance_results.criterion: Direct call fallback remains available
acceptance_results.evidence: Navigation, forms, tool footer, and failure copy retain tel links.

findings.reason: README now matches Next 16.2.6, server lead configuration, safe failure, PWA removal, and explicit production gates without claiming the private runbook is complete.

acceptance_results.criterion: README framework and dependency versions match the audited or replacement lockfile
acceptance_results.evidence: README names Next 16.2.6 and Node 22; lock/build confirm.

acceptance_results.criterion: Setup instructions name required configuration without exposing secrets
acceptance_results.evidence: Durable directory, delivery URL/token, and analytics ID are named with blank examples only.

acceptance_results.criterion: Documentation explicitly states the current lead delivery limitation and points to the controlled verification gate
acceptance_results.evidence: README says production storage/routing/delivery test is required; runbook provides labeled drill.

acceptance_results.criterion: Immediate documentation corrections do not claim the later operational runbook is complete
acceptance_results.evidence: Private owners/destinations, retry automation, and drills are explicit release gates.

findings.reason: Production provider/account/destination evidence is unavailable; only local labeled acceptance, duplicate, failure, and redaction behavior was exercised.

acceptance_results.criterion: One labeled test per distinct transport uses “PROJECT TEARDOWN TEST — DO NOT CONTACT”
acceptance_results.evidence: Local API test used the prefix; no production transport was submitted without configured private authorization/evidence.

acceptance_results.criterion: Sanitized evidence records timestamp, source route, provider acknowledgment, destination, received fields, attribution, and duplicate count
acceptance_results.evidence: Local receipt/source/attribution/duplicate evidence exists; production provider/destination evidence does not.

acceptance_results.criterion: A controlled provider/configuration failure produces a homeowner-safe failure and an operator-visible signal
acceptance_results.evidence: No-store returns 503 safe copy; delivery failure remains pending and logs structured receipt signal.

acceptance_results.criterion: No production customer data or secrets are copied into evidence
acceptance_results.evidence: Only obvious placeholder local test data was used; no production data/secrets entered artifacts.

notes: Exact production destination and provider remain private release configuration.

findings.reason: First-party accepted record is documented and implemented as truth; qualification is measurable; analytics emits PII-free tool start/complete/CTA attribution only.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner names one analytics source of truth and the reconciliation role of every secondary analytics system
acceptance_results.evidence: Accepted first-party record is receipt/qualification truth; Vercel/optional GA are attribution/funnel only.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner defines a qualified lead with measurable minimum fields, service-area fit, service intent, contactability, exclusions, and the event/state that records qualification
acceptance_results.evidence: Schema/isQualifiedLead encode return contact, plausible need, nearby address, accepted state, and test/spam/duplicate exclusions.

acceptance_results.criterion: Event dictionary and funnel documented
acceptance_results.evidence: Runbook documents tool_start/tool_complete/tool_cta and accepted/delivery states.

acceptance_results.criterion: All five tools emit start/complete/CTA with no PII
acceptance_results.evidence: Shared wrapper applies to all five slugs and emits only event plus tool slug.

acceptance_results.criterion: Accepted and routed lead states are measurable
acceptance_results.evidence: Records store acceptedAt, qualification, receipt, source/attribution, and pending/acknowledged delivery.

findings.reason: File selection/preview/reuse/success state was removed and replaced by explicit mail-draft instructions requiring the homeowner to attach and send.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner records manual-email containment or real-upload implementation
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner selected temporary manual email containment.

acceptance_results.criterion: Selecting photos never implies transfer unless bytes are actually acknowledged by the receiving system
acceptance_results.evidence: No file input exists; copy says website does not upload or attach.

acceptance_results.criterion: Success appears only after acknowledged receipt or is labeled only as “mail draft opened”
acceptance_results.evidence: No photo success state exists; opening draft is explicitly not sending.

acceptance_results.criterion: Selected files are not cleared before acknowledged transfer or explicit cancellation
acceptance_results.evidence: Manual path has no website file selection or in-memory files.

acceptance_results.criterion: Photo reuse remains separate, unchecked, and recorded only when a photo is actually received
acceptance_results.evidence: No reuse control or grant exists; email text and privacy page state sending grants no reuse.

verification: Playwright asserts mail link and zero file inputs
notes: Future upload lifecycle requirements remain documented, not implemented.

findings.reason: Owner approved broad numeric planning ranges without blocking on missing job data; UI is truthful now, but original calibration/source/back-test criteria remain unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner selects calibrated-range or nonnumeric/qualified-guide path
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner selected retained numeric broad qualified planning ranges with future calibration.

acceptance_results.criterion: Every retained number has source period, sample size, geography, included/excluded scope, and review owner
acceptance_results.evidence: UI discloses uncalibrated status, Omaha area, exclusions, and owner, but source period/sample do not exist.

acceptance_results.criterion: A back-test against representative completed quotes meets an owner-approved error band
acceptance_results.evidence: Representative redacted jobs, quotes, and approved error band were unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: UI never labels a four-bucket lookup personalized, firm, professionally accurate, or market-validated without evidence
acceptance_results.evidence: Playwright confirms Broad Planning Range and Not a quote; authority labels/precise typical anchor removed.

notes: Cost edits are attributed to TRUST-002/CONTENT-002 while calibration remains an external future strengthening gate per owner direction.

findings.reason: Public count now matches ten active profiles, tools use screening/matching/range/reference language, and unsupported treatment/equipment/availability selections were removed or bounded as inquiries.

acceptance_results.criterion: All public counts equal the active canonical dataset count
acceptance_results.evidence: Homepage states 10 profiles and active species component has ten; contradictory 12/24+ claims removed.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tool names and descriptions state lookup/screening/range behavior rather than exact identification or diagnosis
acceptance_results.evidence: Metadata, hub, homepage, and results use matching, screening, broad planning, and reference terminology.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner confirms every marketed service, equipment claim, geographic availability, and response capability
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner approved capable local tree-service positioning; specific equipment, treatment, guarantee, response-time, and unbounded availability claims were removed.

acceptance_results.criterion: Unavailable services cannot be selected or advertised
acceptance_results.evidence: Health/EAB treatment selection removed; storm is an inquiry with availability confirmation; specialized equipment is no longer promised.

notes: Unused duplicate data and dormant hazard implementation were removed; the active five tools and field estimate remain.

findings.reason: Application CSP/frame/referrer/permissions/content-type headers and server size/schema/honeypot/idempotency/rate controls were added, but distributed production abuse behavior, HSTS, provider quotas, and deployed responses remain external.

acceptance_results.criterion: Automated duplicate/burst tests are bounded and produce observable operator signals without blocking ordinary accessible use
acceptance_results.evidence: Duplicate test and in-process 8/10-minute burst boundary exist; distributed production behavior/alert routing is unverified.

acceptance_results.criterion: Production responses verify the approved CSP, frame-ancestors or equivalent, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, and HSTS where TLS ownership permits
acceptance_results.evidence: Local production server captured all except HSTS; deployed TLS-owned response capture is unavailable.

acceptance_results.criterion: Lead payload size, schema, idempotency, and logging/redaction boundaries have failure-path tests
acceptance_results.evidence: Vitest and HTTP checks cover contact/size/idempotency/no-store; structured logs omit contact fields.

acceptance_results.criterion: Provider-side protections and quotas are documented but not treated as substitutes for application controls
acceptance_results.evidence: Runbook states application boundaries; actual selected provider quota/protection is not configured or known.

notes: Security files are attributed to CONV-002/SEC-001 while deployed verification remains blocked.

findings.reason: A bounded default Midwest Roots configuration and pure tested hazard/lead rules exist, duplicated unused datasets were removed, but all five interactive rule sets are not yet extracted into a tenant-neutral router-free kernel.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner records build-boundary-now or defer-until-local-stability; commercialization remains a separate PROD-001 decision
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner selected boundary now and separately deferred commercialization.

acceptance_results.criterion: No tool kernel imports Midwest Roots constants or Next router
acceptance_results.evidence: Pure hazard/lead modules meet this, but species/DIY/ailment/cost rules remain embedded in UI adapters that import routing/business context.

acceptance_results.criterion: Rules are pure and covered by boundary tests
acceptance_results.evidence: Hazard and lead rules are pure/tested; equivalent extraction/tests for all remaining tools are incomplete.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tenant adapter owns branding, locale, leads, analytics, and disclosures
acceptance_results.evidence: siteConfig separates identity, routing/retention, service areas, pricing status, analytics, tool copy, authority, and feature flags with Midwest Roots defaults.

notes: No tenancy, billing, generalized SaaS, or managed-embed runtime was added.

findings.reason: Serwist, service worker source/dependencies, both install manifests, and metadata links were removed; one-release cleanup unregisters workers/deletes caches, but deployed stale-client proof is external.

acceptance_results.criterion: Decision recorded
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner explicitly chose remove PWA installation/offline/cache/update complexity.

acceptance_results.criterion: If retained, install/offline/update tested in supported browsers; if removed, no stale registrations remain
acceptance_results.evidence: New build has no SW registration/output and cleanup runs in Chrome; deployed /sw.js and previously installed client control have not been observed after deployment.

notes: PWA changes are attributed to OPS-001/CONTENT-002 while production stale-control clearance remains a release gate.

findings.reason: Owner chose local stabilization and one later paid managed-embed pilot only; broad platform infrastructure was excluded and pilot thresholds/ownership prerequisites documented.

acceptance_results.criterion: Owner chooses local-only, licensed embed, managed SaaS, or deferral
acceptance_results.evidence: Owner chose stabilize local now, then preserve one paid managed-embed pilot path.

acceptance_results.criterion: Pilot success thresholds and liability/content ownership are written before build
acceptance_results.evidence: README requires named buyer/fee/support/content-liability/update/lead/privacy/exit owners and defines paid journey/lead integrity/renewal threshold before pilot build.

verification: scope/diff review confirms no SaaS, tenancy, billing, or pilot runtime

findings.reason: A complete executable public runbook structure exists, but private backup owner/destinations/provider accounts and production drills cannot be completed without owner operational access.

acceptance_results.criterion: Runbook names system owner, backup owner, destinations, provider accounts, and escalation path without embedding secrets
acceptance_results.evidence: System owner/roles/escalation method are defined; backup owner, selected provider, and private destinations still require owner configuration.

acceptance_results.criterion: A labeled test-lead drill and provider-outage drill can be completed from the runbook
acceptance_results.evidence: Runbook gives exact prefix, evidence fields, success/pending/no-store cases, restoration, and once-only proof steps.

acceptance_results.criterion: Retention, deletion, duplicate reconciliation, alert thresholds, rollback, and provider rotation are documented
acceptance_results.evidence: All topics have explicit procedures and safe rollback boundary.

acceptance_results.criterion: Runbook version facts match the deployed system
acceptance_results.evidence: Facts match current branch implementation; no deployment has occurred and production provider facts are unavailable.

notes: No secrets or production customer records are present.

convergence_findings.title: Rendered homepage retained unsupported authority and response claims
convergence_findings.reason: The first browser snapshot exposed professional-arborist, exact-safety, since-2023, and two-hour callback claims missed by the initial scan; all were replaced with bounded wording.

convergence_findings.title: Failed acceptance could leave an empty idempotency lock
convergence_findings.source: manual lead-store fault and concurrency review
convergence_findings.reason: An error after exclusive key creation could leave an empty key and make future duplicates fail indefinitely; failure now removes the lock and concurrent empty locks fail safely.

convergence_findings.title: Vitest discovered Playwright specifications
convergence_findings.reason: The initial npm test glob loaded Playwright specs in Vitest; the unit script now excludes tests/e2e and each runner passes independently.

convergence_findings.title: Species and location surfaces retained unsupported certainty and service claims
convergence_findings.reason: Species entries still presented unsupported mortality, lifespan, treatment, and tree-level risk certainty; location pages asserted neighborhood conditions, free assessment, permit handling, and equipment knowledge without evidence. The surfaces now use bounded concern language, property-specific planning, municipal confirmation, and estimate-scope wording.
verification: lint passed with 23 warnings and no errors
Playwright 4 tests passed in installed Chrome
